---
id: 0045
title: "Multi-session dispatch: claim protocol, not sync-before-spend"
status: accepted
summary: "Issue #30 ran twice in parallel (~$5-8 wasted, ISSUE-106.md:6) because a session had no way to see another session was already working the issue. Prevention: a visible claim marker (comment/assign) posted when execution starts, cleared on completion — houses as a CLAUDE.md Shipping-section bullet. Sync-before-spend (prose re-fetch) and 'both' rejected: sync only detects post-spend, and prose-only rules have a proven repo-local decay track record (ADR 0042/#107, floored 3x)."
---

# 0045 — Multi-session dispatch: claim protocol, not sync-before-spend

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator.
- Context: A backlog-dispatched session executed issue #30 in full (~$5-8 nested `claude -p` + agent
  time) while PR #97 concurrently closed the same issue on main; the session's clone was
  fresh-at-start but stale-by-use, and the duplicate surfaced only at end-of-session `git fetch`
  (ISSUE-106.md:6). Gap: nothing forces a session to signal it's working an issue, or to detect a
  concurrent claim before spending (ISSUE-106.md:10). Three candidates on the table (ISSUE-106.md:14-16):
  sync-before-spend (prose re-fetch+re-read), claim protocol (visible marker), or both.

## Decision
Claim protocol only: on execution start, post a one-line "in progress (session X)" issue comment
(or assign); clear it on completion. Houses as a new CLAUDE.md Shipping-section bullet, paired with
the foreground-wait session-ops lesson (ISSUE-106.md:18). Sync-before-spend is NOT added, alone or
alongside claim.

## Justification
Claim is the only one of the three that can PREVENT the spend rather than detect it after: a second
session checking for an existing claim before dispatch stops before spending anything, closing the
race at its narrowest point (the gap between session A starting and A's comment posting) instead of
its widest (session A's full ~$5-8 run). Sync-before-spend re-checks at fixed points (dispatch-time,
pre-push) but the observed incident's actual failure was a race that opened AFTER a clean
dispatch-time check (#30 read `open`) and closed only via an end-of-session `git fetch` — i.e., the
ad hoc practice ISSUE-106 wants formalized already caught this incident, post-spend; formalizing it
saves the wasted PR, not the wasted money (premortem #3). Prose-only rules also have a proven
repo-local decay track record: ADR 0042 (docs/decisions/0042-experiment-methodology-and-prespend-discipline.md:13,
:38) shows a prose-only pre-reg fix still floored a THIRD time even after ADR 0025 had settled it —
sync-before-spend is the same shape (a rule a dispatched session must remember to re-apply
mid-execution, nothing structural checking it happened; ISSUE-106.md:14 names this itself). Adding
sync on top of claim ("both") stacks that already-weak, detect-only layer for a failure mode (a
short, unclaimed collision) not yet evidenced anywhere in this corpus — the one recorded incident is
the long-running/claimed shape claim already covers — so "both" is process weight ahead of a second
confirmed failure mode, the gold-plating CLAUDE.md's muda section warns against.

## Assumptions
- [verified] incident anatomy: dispatch-time read `open`, ~$5-8 long-running spend, duplicate
  surfaced only at end-of-session `git fetch` — ISSUE-106.md:6.
- [verified] prose-only fix decay precedent in this repo: pre-reg metric rule floored a 3rd time
  post-settlement — docs/decisions/0042-experiment-methodology-and-prespend-discipline.md:13,38.
- [checkable] CLAUDE.md gains the claim-protocol bullet + foreground-wait lesson; `adr-lint` and
  `validate.py` stay green post-edit — owner: verifier at implementation.
- [unverifiable] WEAKEST — dispatch instructions grant the session issue-write permission in the
  backlog-dispatch mode that caused this incident, so the claim can actually be posted/checked —
  ISSUE-106.md:15 states dispatch instructions "sometimes withhold" this ("don't change the
  issues"). REOPEN-IF a backlog-dispatch session is denied issue-write permission and a collision
  recurs inside that gap — the failure is silent (no comment posted, no error), so it surfaces only
  as a repeat duplicate-spend incident, not a visible break.

## Rejected alternatives
- Sync-before-spend alone — detects after the spend, not before (premortem #3); same
  enforcement shape as the pre-reg rule that already decayed 3x (ADR 0042).
- Both (sync + claim) — the one observed incident is long-running/claimed, not
  short/unclaimed; stacking sync buys coverage for an unconfirmed second failure mode, which is
  gold-plate, not defense-in-depth, until a short/unclaimed collision is actually observed.

## Revisit triggers
- A backlog-dispatch session is denied issue-write permission AND a collision recurs in that gap →
  add sync-before-spend as a fallback second layer (graduate to "both").
- A future collision is the short/unclaimed shape (not long-running) → re-examine whether
  sync-before-spend should be added for that distinct failure mode.
